meta.title: Incident Rate Report — Plant 2, Q1 2026
meta.subtitle: TRIR · DART · LTIFR · OSHA-standard bases · computed by the incident_rates engine
meta.author: HSE Performance Lead
meta.classification: Internal — performance figures; competent-person review required
meta.deid_notice: All incident detail behind these figures is de-identified AND aggregated — only period counts and standard rates appear. No individual case, injured party, exact date+location, or small (<5) injury cell is identifiable, and no re-identification key appears. Figures are decision-support; a competent person must review them.

sections.text: Lagging incident rates for the period

All three rates below are computed deterministically by the shared incident_rates engine from the period's recordable counts and the actual hours worked — they are NOT estimated in prose. Each figure is reproducible: the same inputs through the same code return the same number. The denominator is the real hours-to-date for this exact period (290,000 h), not annualized. The bases are OSHA-standard engine constants (200,000 for TRIR/DART, 1,000,000 for LTIFR), never recomputed here.

Each rate is shown with the exact formula and inputs the engine used, so a reviewer can re-run the calculation and reproduce the figure to the dp. The value column carries the engine's returned number verbatim.

sections.caption: Rates are period-actual (not annualized). Values are produced by incident_rates and presented verbatim; no figure is computed in-prompt.

sections.text: Computed rates are compared against an industry benchmark ONLY when the benchmark carries its publishing body + year + sector; a bare comparator is flagged [GAP], never invented. A lower rate is better.

sections.caption: Benchmark deltas from incident_rates.benchmark_delta; an unsourced comparator is recorded [GAP], never used.

sections.text: Severity rate is NOT computed: there is no validated severity-rate engine in v1.0, so it is recorded [GAP] rather than estimated in-prompt (D-03).

A rate is meaningless without its exposure base. If hours worked is missing or zero, the calculator surfaces an honest error and produces NO rate — it never emits a fake 0.0, and it never annualizes a partial period.

Provenance: TRIR/DART/LTIFR produced by scripts/hse_components/incident_rates (compute_all / trir / dart / ltifr); benchmark deltas by benchmark_delta. Inputs de-identified and aggregated before computation. Decision-support only — a competent person must review these figures.
